title: AEP v1.2 F23 Validator Adversary Mode Record
description: v1.2 immune-system primitive. Every validator must be tested by an adversary that tries to make a bad packet pass (operator source.md L17 + L69 + L180-186). Operator framing: 'If a validator cannot catch deliberate corruption, it does not deserve authority.' Lineage EXTENDS AFL + honggfuzz + Hypothesis mutation testing + property-based testing per operator L17. A4 MEDIUM closure: adversary recursion stops at depth_2; reuses v1.1 F14 RaterQuorumAttestation for dispute resolution; do NOT invent meta-adversary per sec50 EH Law-3. 7-mutation-class enum verbatim from operator L17. Composes_with: v1.1 F13 ClaimRuntimeFalsifier + v1.1 F16 AttackClass registry.

validator_version.description: Validator semver or commit sha at attack time.

mutation_class.description: Operator L17 verbatim 7-mutation-class enum. Schema-additive-only: future mutation classes added via v1.2.1+ amendment.

mutation_input_sha256.description: sha256 of the mutated packet input. Enables re-execution + dispute resolution.

mutation_description.description: Plain-language description of the mutation. Example for hash_flip: 'Flipped one byte in sources[0].sha256 to corrupt the hash; expected validator to detect via re-hash check.'

validator_outcome_on_mutation.description: What the validator actually did when run against the mutated packet. caught = correctly rejected; missed = wrongly accepted (sandbox theater); errored = crashed; timed_out = exceeded budget.

validator_should_have_caught.description: Ground truth: did the mutation introduce a defect that should have been caught? True for all 7 mutation classes by construction (each mutation creates a known-bad packet).

adversary_verdict.description: Combination of validator_outcome_on_mutation + validator_should_have_caught. validator_passes if outcome=='caught' && should_have_caught=='true'. validator_fails if outcome=='missed' && should_have_caught=='true'. inconclusive on errored/timed_out (requires F14 rater_quorum tiebreaker).

rationale.description: If status != NO_DOWNGRADE, plain-language rationale. Plan sec3 (Phase 3 acceptance): at least one real downgrade must be authored — otherwise the layer is decorative per sec56.

depth.description: A4 MEDIUM closure HARD-CONSTRAINED. Adversary recursion depth. depth=1 = adversary attacks validator. depth=2 = adversary attacks adversary's mutation tooling. depth >=3 REJECTED by validator; use F14 rater_quorum instead.

stop_condition_active.description: True when depth==2 has been reached and further recursion is structurally blocked.

required_when_inconclusive.description: A4 closure: when adversary_verdict == validator_inconclusive, F14 quorum becomes load-bearing. >=3 distinct principals attest the mutation actually corrupted the packet.

quorum_records_referenced.description: F14 RaterQuorumAttestation record ids that ratified the dispute.

external_precedents.description: F18 lineage. F23 ships EXTENDS: AFL (American Fuzzy Lop), honggfuzz, Hypothesis (Python property-based testing). Operator L17 cite.

examples.mutation_description: Flipped one byte in sources[0].sha256 (first nibble 'a' -> 'b') to corrupt the hash; expected validator to detect via re-hash check.

validator_downgrade_recommendation.rationale: Validator correctly re-hashed sources[0] against stored sha256 and rejected the mutation.
